title: Smallanthus oaxacanus
intro: Smallanthus oaxacanus, also known as Mexican dandelion or Hierba de San Andrés, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, diabetes, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Smallanthus oaxacanus include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal supplements, often in combination with other plants, to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Smallanthus oaxacanus is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This family is one of the largest and most widespread plant families in the world. Within the Asteraceae family, Smallanthus oaxacanus is further classified as a member of the subfamily Asteroideae. The subfamily Asteroideae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many common species such as daisies and sunflowers. At the genus and species level, Smallanthus oaxacanus is specifically classified as Smallanthus oaxacanus, a unique and relatively rare species.
